mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.8 million units signals a structural housing market collapse that drags consumer spending and credit growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with declining non-farm payrolls indicates a broad labor market deterioration that reduces household income and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Joint signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) or returns are positive when both signals align negatively.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening business activity, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in > 60% of declining INDPRO months during credit crunch periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI exceeds 295.0, inflation expectations outpace nominal yields, prompting equity investors to demand higher real risk premiums and sell equities, generating negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL > 295.0.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth on 60-day returns falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rising above 4.0% during a financial stress period signals a structural labor market breakdown that amplifies recession fears and drives sustained negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0 during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and real sales of final goods indicate a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that forces immediate inventory corrections and triggers sharp negative 5-day equity moves.
failure_condition: Joint decline signal yields Rank IC < 0.02 or positive 5-day returns in > 60% of co-declining months.
